name: dumbbell forward lunge triceps extension
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a dumbbell in each hand.
Take a step forward with your right foot, lowering your body into a lunge position.
Keep your back straight and your chest up.
Extend your arms straight overhead, keeping your elbows close to your ears.
Lower the dumbbells behind your head by bending your elbows.
Pause for a moment, then straighten your arms to return to the starting position.
Repeat the movement for the desired number of repetitions, then switch legs and repeat.

Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a dumbbell in each hand. Take a step forward with your right foot, lowering your body into a lunge position. Keep your back straight and your chest up. Extend your arms straight overhead, keeping your elbows close to your ears. Lower the dumbbells behind your head by bending your elbows. Pause for a moment, then straighten your arms to return to the starting position. Repeat the movement for the desired number of repetitions, then switch legs and repeat.

conditionNotes.note: Keep load symmetrical and stop at positions that create sharp pain or twisting.
Use pain-free range of motion, slower tempo, and lighter progressions when joints feel irritated.
Weight-bearing resistance exercises are beneficial for bone density when load is appropriate.
Maintain neutral spine and avoid positions that aggravate the lower back.
Maintain consistent breathing throughout. Avoid breath-holding.
Both aerobic and resistance training improve insulin sensitivity. Monitor blood sugar before, during, and after sessions. Have a fast-acting carb available.
Rhythm-based exercises and large-amplitude movements are beneficial. Focus on motor confidence.
Focus on muscle co-contraction and joint stability. Avoid locking out joints passively.
Functional patterns are generally safe. Prioritize pelvic floor awareness and avoid Valsalva.
Start light with higher reps and shorter ROM. Progress load gradually as pain-free capacity increases.
